findings.title: Default access logging now allocates and copies an extra formatted request string
findings.impact: When the sampler is disabled or all rates fold to pass-through, IsCurrentRequestLogSampled() returns true for every request trace, so LogPerformance() calls FormatAccessReqMsg(), builds a separate ostringstream/string, appends logSampled:true, then copies that result into the outer logStream for every access record. This adds an extra allocation/copy and increases emitted access-log payload on the default hot access-log path.
findings.fix: Keep the pass-through path as a direct append: either return false when the sampler is disabled unless an actual admitted decision exists, or change FormatAccessReqMsg() back to append into the caller's stream and only allocate/insert the marker when a real sampled-in decision needs it.

findings.title: Repeated protobuf keys are copied into a vector before formatting
findings.impact: PROTO_REF access records now allocate a std::vector and copy every protobuf key string before calling objectKeysToString(keys). Worker batch paths such as Exist/DeleteAllCopy/MultiCreate pass RepeatedPtrField values through this path, so sampled-in access logs pay an avoidable O(n) allocation and string-copy cost on batch request handling.
findings.fix: Use the new objectKeysToString(const google::protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<std::string>&) overload directly from the PROTO_REF branch, or add a summary variant for protobuf fields when only abbreviated output is needed.
